
# Coding Standards

- Prefer explicit types over `var` unless the type is obvious.
- Keep methods small, readable, and intention‑revealing.
- Use guard clauses instead of nested conditionals.
- Avoid static state unless architecturally justified.
- Prefer immutability where practical.
- Keep naming consistent, descriptive, and aligned with project conventions.
- Avoid speculative abstractions; refactor only when needed.
- Use expression‑bodied members sparingly; clarity comes first.
